title: pittosporum malaxanii
intro: Pittosporum malaxanii, also known as Kohuhu, is a native New Zealand plant that belongs to the Pittosporaceae family. Traditionally, Kohuhu has been used in Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions such as skin infections and wounds. The plant's leaves, bark, and roots have been used in herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and ointments, to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and soothe skin irritations. Regular consumption of Kohuhu is believed to provide health benefits, including improving digestion, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues, although more research is needed to confirm these claims.
